unit: U2 — Domain mode files + paired rubrics

contract.goal: Ship the 5 domain mode files at plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/ and their 5 paired rubric files at plugins/jelly-guild/rubrics/. The mode files are read by personality subagents at dispatch time to construct the WHAT axis of their identity; the rubrics are the same conceptual content formatted for Outcomes auto-provisioned grader consumption. Manually synced in v1.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/{composition,naming,abstraction,testing,a11y}.md exist — plain markdown (no YAML frontmatter; modes are content, not subagents) with sections: # Domain: <name>, ## Scope, ## Concerns, ## Antipattern catalog, ## Good patterns, ## Vocabulary, ## Cross-domain notes.

plugins/jelly-guild/rubrics/{composition,naming,abstraction,testing,a11y}.md exist — markdown shaped for Outcomes-grader consumption: # Rubric: <name>, ## Scope (with cross-reference back to the paired mode), ## Criteria (numbered with PASS/FAIL definitions), ## Severity (blocking vs advisory split), ## Evidence shape.
Each domain mode is architecture-shaped, not language-specific (language-specific antipatterns ride in the per-task brief at dispatch time per PLAN.md § Decisions log 2026-05-25).
Each rubric is the same conceptual content as its paired mode (manually synced in v1 per PLAN.md decision; mode file is canonical on drift).
npm test passes (937 tests; +0 — modes + rubrics are declarative content, no test changes).

Files encode the substrate's voice (composition over configuration, semantic over literal, naming-is-architecture, three-similar-lines, test-the-boundary, design-for-inclusion-from-start) rather than generic-best-practices boilerplate.
rules_applied: PLAN.md § Phase 1.2 Exit (5 domain mode files + 5 paired rubrics + manual sync + architecture-shaped).
PLAN.md § Decisions log 2026-05-25 'Domains v1 (5 files)' — composition, naming, abstraction, testing, a11y as the canonical set.
PLAN.md § Decisions log 2026-05-25 'Paired rubrics' — same conceptual content, two formats, manually synced.
Global CLAUDE.md design-systems philosophy informs the substrate's voice (composition over configuration; semantic over literal; naming-is-architecture; etc.).
Pattern A for checkin lifecycle (complete checkin written once at unit end).
Substrate-only unit → panel auto-derivation returns evaluator-contract-fit only (modes + rubrics are markdown content; no domain evaluator applies).
disqualifiers: Adding personality subagent files (plugins/jelly-guild/agents/*.md) — U4 territory.
Adding phase mode files (plugins/jelly-guild/modes/phases/*.md) — U3 territory.
Adding the CLAUDE.md template — U5 territory.
Adding YAML frontmatter to mode or rubric files (they are read-only content, not subagent definitions; frontmatter would be a category error).
Adding language-specific antipatterns (TypeScript-specific naming rules, React-specific composition patterns). The modes are architecture-portable.
Adding tool references in mode bodies (modes are content read by personalities, not subagent definitions).
Modifying evaluator-base.md or other existing guild agents.
inputs: projects/2026-05-25-jelly/PLAN.md § Phase 1.2 (lines 57-77) + § Decisions log (lines 156-164 covering the three-axis decomp and Domains v1 + Paired rubrics decisions).
User's global CLAUDE.md (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) for design-systems philosophy + collaboration voice.
Existing plugins/guild/agents/evaluator-{a11y,naming,test-unit}.md (reference shape for antipattern catalog structure, NOT for direct copy — those are evaluator subagents; modes are content).

actions: Surveyed existing plugins/guild/agents/evaluator-{a11y,naming,test-unit,react-api,tokens}.md for antipattern-catalog shape reference (severity classification, blocking-vs-advisory, evidence shape). Mode files reuse the antipattern-catalog organizational pattern but drop the evaluator-specific scaffolding (Process steps, CLI signals, inherited base contract).

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/composition.md (129 lines): opens with 'Composition over configuration' framing from user's stated design-systems philosophy. Concerns: composition over configuration, single responsibility, rails+escape-hatches, coupling, reusability-without-contortion. 7-entry antipattern catalog (configuration-explosion, monolithic-primitive, internal-switches-as-variants, primitives-that-do-not-compose, god-object, tight-coupling-via-shared-state, layered-abstractions-without-escape-hatches). Good patterns + vocabulary + cross-domain notes referencing abstraction/naming/testing/a11y.

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/naming.md (162 lines): opens with 'Naming is architecture. Find cohesion always.' (verbatim from user's CLAUDE.md design-systems framing). Concerns: semantic-over-literal, vocabulary-cohesion, predicate-form-booleans, no-implementation-in-identifier, public-surface-abbreviations, file-and-directory-conventions. 9-entry antipattern catalog. Cross-domain notes connect to composition (primitives need clear individual names) and abstraction (a difficult name signals premature abstraction).

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/abstraction.md (147 lines): opens with 'Three similar lines is fine. Premature abstraction is worse than duplication.' (the user's stated three-similar-lines rule). 7-entry antipattern catalog (single-use-abstraction, speculative-parameter, over-DRY, wrapper-without-value, half-finished-abstraction, layered-indirection, premature-generic). Three-similar-lines and rule-of-three explicit in the Good patterns section.

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/testing.md (158 lines): opens with 'Test the boundary, not the implementation. ... A passing test that asserts the wrong thing is worse than no test.' (the user's stated test-the-boundary principle). 9-entry antipattern catalog. References user's 'integration tests must hit a real database, not mocks' memory (feedback_loom_phase_and_checkin_gaps relative — actually that's the loom-substrate one; the relevant memory here is the integration-test-mock-vs-real one from the user's voice). Tier-choice + mock-boundary + flake-isolation are the load-bearing principles.

Wrote plugins/jelly-guild/modes/domains/a11y.md (193 lines): the longest mode because a11y has the most concrete catalog entries. Opens with 'Designed for inclusion from the start over retrofitting it.' Scope explicitly addresses platform portability ('A11y is web-flavored ... but the architecture-level concerns port to other platforms') to satisfy the architecture-shaped acceptance criterion. 10-entry antipattern catalog. Cross-domain notes connect to composition (composable primitives can encode a11y at primitive level) and naming (ARIA values are names too).

Wrote 5 rubrics at plugins/jelly-guild/rubrics/{composition,naming,abstraction,testing,a11y}.md (97-140 lines each): each opens with a `## Scope` cross-reference to the paired mode file ('The mode file at <path> is the prose form of this same content. The two are manually synced in v1; if you find drift, the mode is canonical.'). Criteria are PASS/FAIL pairs matching the mode's antipattern entries 1:1. Severity section separates blocking from advisory with the diff-only-worsening standard. Evidence shape section names what the grader should cite per finding.

Verified: find plugins/jelly-guild -type f returns 16 files (1 plugin.json + 5 .gitkeep from U1 + 10 new .md). Total new content: 1365 lines across 10 files. npm test → 937 passed across 67 files (unchanged from U1). Panel (evaluator-contract-fit, auto-derived): approved on first run; no findings.

changes_since_previous: Second unit of Phase 1.2 (jelly-guild substrate); composes on U1's scaffold. With this unit committed, the WHAT axis of the three-axis decomposition is complete (5 domain modes + 5 paired rubrics). U3 (phase modes — WHEN axis) and U4 (personality subagents — HOW axis) follow; U5 (CLAUDE.md template + composition verification) caps the phase. Carries lagged pr-opened event for PR #54 in events.jsonl (standard substrate pattern per memory feedback_loom_pr_opened_orphan_and_guild_spec_path).
notes_for_pr: Voice cohesion check: each mode opens with a stated principle drawn from the user's global CLAUDE.md design-systems framing (composition over configuration; naming is architecture; three-similar-lines is fine; test the boundary; design for inclusion from the start). These are the substrate's load-bearing opinions, not generic best-practices boilerplate.

Architecture-shaped check: 4 of 5 modes are platform-agnostic (composition, naming, abstraction, testing). a11y.md is intrinsically web-flavored (HTML + ARIA are its concrete patterns) but the Scope section's paragraph 2 explicitly addresses portability to other platforms (iOS UIAccessibility, Android TalkBack, terminal UIs with reduced-motion). Acceptable per PLAN.md's 'architecture-shaped' constraint — the architecture-level concerns port even when the markup-level patterns don't.

Manual-sync pairing: each rubric's Scope section cites the paired mode path verbatim and names the mode-canonical-on-drift rule. v1 acceptance is manual coupling per PLAN.md decision; a v2 follow-up could derive both formats from a single source.

Cross-domain notes: every mode includes a 'Cross-domain notes' section that names the other 4 domains by their contract names and describes overlap shape. This makes the cross-cutting nature explicit so personality subagents reading multiple mode files at dispatch get a coherent picture rather than five disconnected views.

Diff-only-worsening standard: every rubric's Severity section uses 'the diff itself introduces the antipattern' vs 'inherited and the diff doesn't worsen it' as the blocking-vs-advisory boundary. This is the substrate-canonical lens for evaluator findings; the rubrics encode it consistently so the auto-provisioned grader applies it uniformly.
Substrate-state carry-over: lagged pr-opened event for PR #54 in events.jsonl rides with this commit per the standard pattern.

U3 (phase modes) follows. Phase modes are READ alongside domain modes by personality subagents — the dispatch brief names one personality + one domain + one phase, and the personality reads the three mode files to construct its task identity.
